contribution.title: Kerr-Newman solution
contribution.description: Generalized the Kerr solution to include electric charge, producing the most general black hole solution in Einstein-Maxwell theory.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Fourth metric in our DIM_METRIC table (after Schwarzschild, Kerr, Kerr-Newman, Kerr-deSitter).

contribution.title: Newman-Penrose formalism
contribution.description: Tetrad-based formulation of general relativity, well-suited for studying gravitational radiation and algebraically special spacetimes.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Useful for perturbation theory and ringdown modules.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Use null tetrads and complex coordinates to expose hidden structure.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What does this spacetime look like in a null tetrad frame?
Which Weyl scalars vanish, and what does that mean physically?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: The NP formalism is powerful but obscures intuition; do not impose it where Schwarzschild coordinates make a calculation trivial.

reference.citation: Newman, E. T., Janis, A. I. (1965). Note on the Kerr spinning-particle metric. J. Math. Phys. 6, 915.
Newman, E. T., Penrose, R. (1962). An approach to gravitational radiation by a method of spin coefficients. J. Math. Phys. 3, 566.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Implementing charged black holes, the Kerr-Newman metric, or perturbation theory using Weyl scalars.
how_to_apply.cautions: Sign conventions vary between sources. Cite the convention you adopt.
